ksa_text: Ability to evaluate data sources for relevance, reliability, completeness, and objectivity to determine how source limitations affect analytical conclusions and data fitness.

dcwf.top_element_label: Identify functional- and security-related features to find opportunities for new capability development to exploit or mitigate vulnerabilities.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

top_anchors.element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology
Identify functional- and security-related features to find opportunities for new capability development to exploit or mitigate vulnerabilities.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Quality falls outside the primary competency scope of the AI CoE Leader. This ability KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Data Quality truly out-of-scope for the AI CoE Leader? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Data Quality as a core or recommended competency for the AI CoE Leader. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Data Quality falls outside the established competency profile for the AI CoE Leader. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Data Quality is outside the competency scope of the AI CoE Leader. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
